release_review.summary: Redeven adopts published Flowersec v0.20.2 and Floe Webapp v0.37.1 communication hardening while retaining the published Floret v0.4.0 model-directed concurrency integration, without changing the Runtime Service compatibility window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1; the compatibility epoch remains 5 and minimum Desktop/Runtime versions remain v0.7.0

Remote Flowersec Direct and Tunnel connections explicitly require TLS, Local UI Direct connections explicitly allow plaintext only for canonical loopback authorities, and the Docker integration client uses the same loopback-only exception

Flowersec Go and TypeScript v0.20.2 and Floe Webapp v0.37.1 are consumed only from published artifacts with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, package-manager link, workspace alias, or local sibling path wiring

Acknowledged Yamux liveness, 64 KiB encrypted-record chunks, WebSocket watermarks, Yamux stream and receive-memory limits, and bounded RPC workers and queues change transport resource behavior without changing Redeven RPC type IDs or payload schemas

Env App reconnects use refreshable ArtifactSource acquire functions and stable reconnect config objects so credentials remain one-use and trace context survives hard reconnects without changing session metadata or Runtime Service envelopes

The prior flower-model-directed-tool-concurrency review remains represented in this release: model tool calls execute concurrently through Floret while the Flower approval queue and Permission snapshots preserve their existing product and integrity boundaries
Floret is consumed only from the published github.com/floegence/floret v0.4.0 module with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, package-manager link, or local sibling path wiring

Model tool calls emitted in one response execute concurrently inside the published Floret lifecycle while transcript ordering, cancellation, permission, approval, audit, and control-signal boundaries remain internal to Flower and Floret

Provider parallel_tool_calls wire enablement is limited to documented official endpoints and only affects model request generation; it does not participate in execution scheduling or Runtime Service request and response schemas

Flower serializes user approval interaction through thread live state with queue generation, revision, and action compare-and-swap fields while approved handlers may overlap; this product state does not change Runtime Service control methods
Permission snapshots use a current v2 view while preserving exact v1 integrity verification and historical records; no Runtime Service permission or persistence envelope changes

Desktop attach/start, Local UI health, structured startup reports, sys.ping, Env App maintenance UI, gateway lifecycle, SSH runtime bootstrap, and installer version discovery remain compatible with the existing v0.7.0 minimum window
release_review.same_window_rationale: The compatibility window is unchanged because Flowersec v0.20.2 and Floe Webapp v0.37.1 preserve ConnectArtifact v1, E2EE record framing, Yamux wire behavior, Redeven RPC type IDs and payloads, and the redeven-runtime-v1 service contract while hardening handshake consumption, session termination, backpressure, resource ownership, and response-header policy. Floret v0.4.0 batch execution, provider request shaping, Flower approval queue state, and permission snapshot migration remain internal AI runtime and product live-state behavior. Together these changes do not alter Runtime Service requests, responses, control methods, attach/start handshakes, structured startup reports, sys.ping semantics, Local UI health, Env App maintenance routes, gateway lifecycle messages, SSH runtime bootstrap, or installer version discovery. Existing v0.7.0 Desktop and Runtime binaries therefore remain compatible.
